skill.name: gws-keep
skill.description: Use the Google Workspace CLI to inspect and run Google Keep note, attachment, and permission commands.
skill.summary: Manage Google Keep notes, attachments, and permissions through the gws CLI.
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged command execution, path traversal, and weak crypto patterns, but review found the command matches are Markdown examples for the intended gws CLI and the weak crypto matches are text false positives. The skill is not malicious, but it should be published with a warning because it can access authenticated Google Keep data, download attachments, manage permissions, and delete notes.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Google Keep Data Operations
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE for sensitive data impact. The skill documents commands that can download attachments, create notes, delete notes, get notes, list notes, and operate on permissions. These are legitimate Google Keep capabilities, but they can expose or modify authenticated user data. Confidence: 0.86. Confidence reasoning: The line evidence directly lists Keep data operations, including attachment download and note deletion. No malicious destination or hidden exfiltration path was found.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line evidence directly lists Keep data operations, including attachment download and note deletion. No malicious destination or hidden exfiltration path was found.

low_findings.title: External Command Findings Are Intended CLI Examples
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for command injection. The flagged lines show fixed Markdown examples for the gws CLI, including help and schema discovery commands. They do not build shell commands from user-controlled input. Confidence: 0.94. Confidence reasoning: The snippets are static documentation examples and no dynamic command construction appears in the scanned file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The snippets are static documentation examples and no dynamic command construction appears in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Relative Shared Skill Reference Is Not Path Traversal Code
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for filesystem path traversal. The relative path points to a sibling Google Workspace shared skill document for authentication and security rules. It is a documentation dependency, not executable file access or unsanitized path handling. Confidence: 0.88. Confidence reasoning: The evidence is a prerequisite sentence, and the scanned file contains no code that opens or writes paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is a prerequisite sentence, and the scanned file contains no code that opens or writes paths.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Text False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE for weak cryptography. Lines 3 and 33 contain natural language metadata and pagination text, not cryptographic algorithms or implementations. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, or other weak cryptographic use in the scanned file. Confidence: 0.97. Confidence reasoning: Manual review of both flagged lines found only descriptive prose and no crypto API, digest generation, or security-sensitive hashing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of both flagged lines found only descriptive prose and no crypto API, digest generation, or security-sensitive hashing.

dangerous_patterns.title: Destructive Note Deletion Capability
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill exposes a documented delete operation that immediately removes a Google Keep note and cannot be undone. This is an intended capability, but users should confirm note IDs and ownership before running it. Confidence: 0.91. Confidence reasoning: The line explicitly states the delete behavior and irreversible impact.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The line explicitly states the delete behavior and irreversible impact.

dangerous_patterns.title: Attachment Download Capability
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill exposes media download for Keep attachments. This can write or reveal user data and should be limited to attachments the user explicitly requests. Confidence: 0.84. Confidence reasoning: The line documents attachment media download through the Keep resource, but no unsafe destination path is specified.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The line documents attachment media download through the Keep resource, but no unsafe destination path is specified.

content.value_statement: Google Keep work often requires precise note lookup, attachment handling, and permission checks. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through gws CLI commands for Keep tasks.
actual_capabilities: Shows the base gws keep command pattern for Keep resources and methods.
Lists supported Keep resources for media, notes, and note permissions.
Guides discovery of required parameters with gws keep help and gws schema.
Documents note operations for create, delete, get, list, and permissions.
Documents media download behavior for Google Keep attachments.
limitations: Requires the gws CLI and authenticated Google Workspace access.
Depends on a separate shared Google Workspace skill for auth and global flags.
Does not include examples for every parameter or response shape.
Cannot restore notes after a successful delete operation.

use_cases.title: Review personal Keep notes
use_cases.description: List notes, inspect details, and locate the note that matches a specific task or topic.

use_cases.title: Prepare team note audits
use_cases.description: Check note permissions before sharing, cleanup, or ownership review work.

use_cases.title: Handle Keep attachments
use_cases.description: Identify attachment requirements and download media when the user has requested a specific file.

prompt_templates.title: List recent notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the gws Keep skill to inspect the list command, then list my recent Google Keep notes with a small page size.

prompt_templates.title: Create a note
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the gws Keep skill to inspect the create command, then create a Google Keep note with this title and text: [provide title and body].

prompt_templates.title: Download an attachment
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the gws Keep skill to inspect the media download command, confirm the required attachment identifiers, then download only the attachment I specify.

prompt_templates.title: Audit permissions before deletion
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the gws Keep skill to get the note, review permissions, summarize who has access, and ask for confirmation before deleting anything.

output_examples.input: List my Keep notes related to travel planning.
output_examples.output: A short list of matching notes with titles, identifiers, update context, and a prompt to inspect one selected note.

output_examples.input: Check who can access this shared note.
output_examples.output: A permissions summary that names owners or collaborators when available and flags access changes that need confirmation.

output_examples.input: Delete this outdated Keep note after review.
output_examples.output: A confirmation request that explains deletion is immediate and cannot be undone before the delete command is run.

best_practices: Run gws schema before each unfamiliar Keep method to confirm required parameters.
Use small page sizes when listing notes to reduce accidental data exposure.
Ask for explicit confirmation before downloading attachments, changing permissions, or deleting notes.
anti_patterns: Do not delete notes based only on a title match without checking the note ID.
Do not download all attachments when the user requested one specific file.
Do not share raw note content or permissions in public logs or unrelated outputs.

faq.question: Does this skill require Google authentication?
faq.answer: Yes. It relies on the gws CLI and the authenticated Google Workspace account available to the agent.

faq.question: Can it create Google Keep notes?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents the notes create method and tells the agent to inspect the schema before use.

faq.question: Can it delete notes?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents note deletion, and deletion is immediate and cannot be undone.

faq.question: Can it manage note permissions?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill identifies permissions as a notes resource, but exact parameters should be checked with gws schema.

faq.question: Does it include full command examples for every task?
faq.answer: No. It provides resource coverage and discovery commands, then expects schema inspection for precise flags.

faq.question: Is the static security risk confirmed as malware?
faq.answer: No. The risky patterns are mostly documentation examples, but authenticated Keep data access still needs careful use.
